mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-occurring positive deviations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during easing periods yield negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows zero predictive alpha after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal strengthening economic activity and higher corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales expansion confirm broad-based demand strength, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of aligned periods, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals manufacturing contraction and impending earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle periods produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of instances, or the signal shows no predictive power after adjusting for sector rotation effects.
